title: Cockpit layout planner and Herdr adapter methods
goal: Introduce a small cockpit layout planning interface and Herdr adapter methods that can place dashboard and worker panes intentionally instead of relying on ad hoc split order.
acceptance: Add a tested layout planner for the v2 cockpit contract: dashboard right, worker region left, stable worker slot names for 1-4 workers, and no default Operator column.

Add Herdr adapter operations needed by the planner, such as dashboard pane creation or reuse, placeholder/root-pane cleanup, worker pane movement or slot placement, and layout inspection, while keeping the adapter as the only Herdr-specific implementation seam.

Existing cockpit open/fallback semantics remain non-fatal visibility behavior; Herdr layout failures must not change worker authorization, verification, merge, handoff readiness, or run terminal status by themselves.
Record layout-related incidents/events as observability metadata only; do not make Herdr pane text, scrollback, metadata, or Pi display state correctness evidence.
must_ask_if: The layout planner needs a public CLI surface or persistent config change beyond an internal experimental/default cockpit behavior.
The implementation would require treating Herdr state as workflow truth.
